rulings.Case Title: LUIS G VAZQUEZ VS. PATRICIA BUITRAGO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF LUIS VAZQUEZ MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS LENNAR CORPORATION, LENNAR HOMES OF CALIFORNIA INC., LENNAR-BVHP LLC, LENNAR ASSOCIATES MANAGEMENT LLC, LENNAR COMMUNITIES, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to fifth cause of action, plaintiff must plead physical injury. As to the sixth cause of action, plaintiff must plead that it expended money due to defendants' acts of unfair competition, lost money or property, has been deneid money to which it has a cognizable claim, or there has been a diminution in value of its assets. Also, plaintiff must provide to the court an original First Amended Complaint so that it may be property filed, entered in the register of action, and scanned. Plaintiff must also provide an order allowing the filing of this first amended complaint nunc pro tunc to December 8, 2009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LIMPERIS VS. JIMMY JEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS ROBERT MCCURN, BAY AREA AFFORDABLE HOUSING LLC DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend, plaintiff fails to oppose the motion on the merits. The court's order regarding the third amended complaint does not limit defendant's response to "answering." CCP section 430.30. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LIMPERIS VS. JIMMY JEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS ROBERT MCCURN, BAY AREA AFFORDABLE HOUSING LLC MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted. Paragraph 1, page 2, lines 4-10: "Jen has been referred... described in this compaint." stricken. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRENA ROTBART VS. T-MOBILE USA,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT T-MOBILE USA, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to first, second, third, fourth, fifth, and seventh causes of action due to pre-emption. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to sixth cause of action for unfair business practices. Plaintiff to plead facts that demonstrate standing and lack of preemption.=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRENA ROTBART VS. T-MOBILE USA,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT T-MOBILE USA, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted, no opposition. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN PALM SOZA VS. TAQUERIA LA CUMBRE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion In Support Of X-Complainant'S Leave To File A First Amended X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 12, CROSS COMPLAINANT TAQUERIA LA CUMBRE, INC. Motion in Support of Cross-Complainant's Leave to File a First Amended Cross-Complaint. Motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is denied without prejudice to being re-noticed if trial date is continued. Prejudice to recently added cross-defendants due to current trial date, and lack of time to complete discovery and file responsive pleadings. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GRANT & WEBER, A CORPORATION VS. MAX FRUMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Quash Or Vacate Judgment And P&A In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT MAX FRUMES Motion For Order to Quash or Vacate Judgment. Off Calendar. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to CCP section 1005. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INMA PENA VS. BLACKROCK INSTITUTIONAL TRUST COMPANY, NA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT BARCLAYS GLOBAL INVESTORS, NA MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication denied as to disability discrimination cause of action. The allegations of this cause of action are within the scope of the administrative investigation which can reasonably be expected to grow out of the charge of disrimination. The inititial administrative complaint references "denied accommodation" and that BGI would not accommodate plaintiff's return from medical leave by reducing her hours." Motion for summary adjudication denied as to retaliation cause of action, failure to shift burden of proof. Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support retaliation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR J. LEMPERT VS. JOHN CHAING
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 7, PLAINTIFF ARTHUR LEMPERT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. Neither the 2006 agreement nor any other admissible evidence establish that plaintiff is the "owner" of the unclaimed property held by defendant and is entitled to recover it. The agreement is silent as to the assets in questin and it is undisputed that plaintiff did not even learn of the uncollected assets until 2007 or 2008. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO COSTA VS. BEN SIRIMANNE et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANTS BEN SIRIMANNE, CSDS AIRCRAFT SALES & LEASING, INC. Motion To Transfer Venue. Is continued to March 5, 2010 per amended notice of motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: 560 HAIGHT STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION INC A VS. 560 HAIGHT STREET, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS GEORGE BIRMINGHAM, THE BIRMINGHAM GROUP, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to ninth cause of action to plead facts about the specific nature of the fraud, facts as to knowledge of the defects, intent to conceal and what misrepresentations were made. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the tenth cause of action. Willful misconduct is not a cause of action. Plaintiff may plead punitive damages related to their other intentional tort and other causes of action. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN CONCRETE CUTTING INC DBA AMERICAN VS. RECYCLED MATERIALS COMPANY INC et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 19, 2010, line 10, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN CONCRETE CUTTING INC. Demurrer To Defendants' Answer. Off Calendar, Amended answer filed February 18, 2010. =(302/CWW)
